data.title: Bursa Opens Tepidly, FBM KLCI Dips Amid Cautious Trading
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia started the trading day on a softer note, with most major indices retreating marginally in early deals.

As at 9.07am, the benchmark FBM KLCI slipped 1.03 points, or 0.06%, to 1,738.51.

The broader indices also recorded early declines: the FBM70 eased 32.37 points, or 0.18%, to 17,537.30, while the F4GBM lost 0.53 of a point, or 0.05%, to 1,025.69. Meanwhile, the FBMEMAS dropped 10.14 points, or 0.08%, to 12,728.06, and the FBMSHA declined 6.02 points, or 0.05%, to 12,215.99.

Most active counters in early trading were HONGSENG, which gained 0.005 to 0.010 on a volume of 1.26 million shares, ZENTECH [S] at 0.005 with 77,865 shares traded, and TWL [S] at 0.025 on 50,771 shares. 99SMART and ARTRONIQ [S] also saw notable activity, with ARTRONIQ up 0.010 to 0.165.

In terms of price movers, MNRB led the gainers, rising 0.240 to 2.700, followed by CHINTEK (+0.140 to 10.580), F&N [S] (+0.100 to 33.000), PETGAS (+0.080 to 18.300), and GOHUB (+0.055 to 0.945).

On the downside, HEIM fell 0.260 to 24.340, MPI [S] dropped 0.260 to 29.520, 99SMART eased 0.110 to 3.840, KEINHIN [S] declined 0.090 to 1.160, and UTDPLT [S] slipped 0.080 to 30.100.

Market watchers said early caution reflects investors digesting regional and global cues while awaiting fresh catalysts to drive trading momentum.

data.title: Tenaga Leads Top Gainers As UTDPLT Slips In Early Trades
data.description:
Tenaga Nasional Bhd (Tenaga) surged to the top of the gainers’ board, rising 46 sen to RM14.42 on robust trading volume of 9.16 million shares.

Petronas Gas Bhd (PetGas) followed with a 38 sen gain to RM18.60, while Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd (DLADY) climbed 32 sen to RM33.28. Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd (Nestlé) advanced 30 sen to RM109.30, and Knusford Bhd (Knusfor) rose 26 sen to 67.5 sen, rounding off the five biggest risers.

On the other end, UTDPLT Bhd led the decliners, falling 36 sen to RM29.82 with 9.52 million shares traded. HEIM Resources Bhd slipped 30 sen to RM24.30, while Allianz Malaysia Bhd lost 24 sen to RM22.26. 99 Speedmart Holdings Bhd (99Smart) retreated 22 sen to RM3.73 on heavy volume of 49 million shares, and ABMB Bhd shed 22 sen to RM5.07, completing the top five losers.

The session reflected a mixed sentiment as investors rotated funds between large-cap gainers and actively traded counters, while cautious trading kept broader market indices largely flat.

data.title: Weekly Recap: Kospi Hits Record High Above 5,500 Before Profit-Taking Pullback
data.description:
South Korean equities ended the week of Feb 9-13 on a mixed note, with the benchmark Kospi hitting fresh record highs before retreating on late profit-taking ahead of the Chinese New Year holiday.

The Kospi rallied strongly in the first half of the week, driven by gains in semiconductor and blue-chip counters, and surged 3.13% on Feb 12 to close at a historic high of 5,522.27, marking its first-ever break above the 5,500 milestone.

Tech heavyweights led the advance, with semiconductor stocks benefitting from sustained optimism over artificial intelligence-driven demand and strong institutional and foreign buying.

However, the rally lost steam by Feb 13 as investors locked in profits ahead of the extended holiday closure. The Kospi slipped 0.28% to finish at 5,507.01, snapping a four-day winning streak despite briefly touching another intraday record above 5,580. Foreign investors turned net sellers late in the week, while retail and institutional investors provided partial support.

Despite the pullback, overall sentiment remained constructive. The index continued to trade near record levels, underpinned by strong momentum in technology shares and robust institutional inflows, highlighting resilience in South Korea’s equity market following its recent surge.

The week ultimately underscored the Kospi’s bullish trajectory, even as short-term consolidation emerged after its historic breakout.

data.title: Roller Coaster Week For Bitcoin, Are The Bearish Signals Over?
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market saw heightened volatility during the week of Feb 9-13, with Bitcoin and Ethereum retreating amid profit-taking and persistent macro uncertainty.

Bitcoin started the week near US$69,800 but struggled to sustain momentum, briefly testing key support levels around US$68,000 before sliding toward the mid-US$66,000 range by Feb 13. Despite intermittent rebounds, the cryptocurrency faced selling pressure tied to broader risk-off sentiment and ahead of key US economic data releases.

Ethereum, meanwhile, mirrored Bitcoin’s trajectory, trading around US$2,100 early in the week before weakening and dipping below the US$2,000 mark at one stage, reflecting continued bearish technical signals and cautious investor sentiment.

Both cryptocurrencies ended the week largely lower or flat compared with earlier gains, underscoring fragile market confidence.

Institutional flows also highlighted caution, with Bitcoin and Ethereum spot exchange-traded funds recording significant net outflows during the week, contributing to downward pressure on prices. Analysts noted the market is in a consolidation phase after recent sharp declines, with prices stabilising but lacking strong bullish catalysts.

Overall, the week reflected a consolidation period for crypto markets, as investors weighed macroeconomic signals, institutional flows and technical levels, leaving Bitcoin and Ethereum trading near key psychological support zones.

data.title: Hong Kong Stock Market Wraps Up Snake  Year In Upbeat Mode
data.description:
Hong Kong stock market ended higher in half-day trading on Monday, wrapping up the Year of Snake on an upbeat note.

The benchmark Hang Seng Index rose 0.52 percent to close at 26,705.94 points. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index gained 0.42 percent to end at 9,070.32 points, and the Hang Seng Tech Index inched up 0.13 percent to 5,367.52 points.

For the Year of the Snake, the Hang Seng Index surged 32 percent from a year earlier, logging its strongest annual performance in eight lunar years. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index expanded 23 percent, and the Hang Seng Tech Index advanced about 14 percent.

Stock trading will resume on Friday following the Chinese Lunar New Year holiday

data.title: STI Starts Week On Softer Footing
data.description:
Singapore shares opened lower on Monday, tracking regional weakness as investors traded cautiously in early deals.

The benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) slipped 15.91 points, or 0.32%, to 4,921.87 as at 9.05am. Market breadth was negative, with 98 decliners outpacing 88 advancers.

Trading activity was moderate in early session, with 107.11 million shares worth S$134.78 million changing hands.

Among the index constituents, DBS Group Holdings edged down 0.05% to S$57.01. OCBC Bank traded at S$21.10, while United Overseas Bank stood at S$38.30. Singtel changed hands at S$4.87 and Genting Singapore was at S$0.765. Transport operator ComfortDelGro traded at S$1.49.

Across broader indices, the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index stood at 1,513.52, while the iEdge S-REIT Index was at 1,109.05.

In derivatives trading, volume reached 11,560 contracts in early trade. The MSCI Singapore Index Futures February contract was last at 453.25, while regional contracts such as Nikkei 225 Index Futures March 2026 traded at 56,950.00.

Currency futures saw USD/SGD February contracts at 1.2636, with INR/USD February futures recording volume of 173 contracts.

Market participants are expected to monitor regional cues and macroeconomic developments for further direction as the session progresses.

data.title: Veteran Analysts Warns Of Bitcoin Crash To Below US$10,000
data.description:
Bitcoin the world’s first decentralised cryptocurrency, was launched in 2009 in the wake of the global financial crisis in 2008. The goal was to give financial autonomy to the people and liberate them from the clutches of Wall Street.

But the way Bitcoin has increasingly become integrated into the mainstream financial system, macroeconomic forces now tend to affect King Crypto’s price trajectory.That is exactly what happened when U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff threat against China led to a massive liquidation in the crypto market on Oct. 10 last year.

Bitcoin had hit its all-time high (ATH) of $126,080 only a few days earlier on Oct. 6 and it is currently trading 45% lower.Earlier this month, Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan listed six major factors behind the crash in the crypto market.

First, long-term crypto investors have been selling to front-run the four-year cycle. The general thesis is that crypto has historically followed four-year cycles, with down years being 2014, 2018, and 2022 previously. So, traders think 2026 is also going to be a down year and are therefore exiting markets after making gains.

Second, though crypto remained the “most exciting, dynamic, and volatile segment” of the market, investors have now flocked to other exciting segments like artificial intelligence (AI) and precious metals.hree, the crypto market took the blow of Trump’s tariff threat alone, as Oct. 10 was followed by a weekend when Wall Street was closed for trading.

Four, Trump has nominated Kevin Warsh to be the next Federal Reserve chairman, who is seen to hold very hawkish views.

Five, there is a significant section of the Bitcoin community that fears the threat of quantum computing is near and not enough is being done to address the risk.

Six, Bitcoin has suffered from the broader market’s risk-off shift.

On Feb. 16, Bloomberg Intelligence strategist Mike McGlone warned that Bitcoin could even drop to $10,000.

While other analysts may talk of a “healthy correction” soon, a further decline is here, he said. But the buy-the-dip mantra since 2008 may be over, he added

The Street

data.title: Market Snapshot: Hang Seng Index Retreats On Tech Sell-Off, Profit-Taking
data.description:
Trading on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange closed out a choppy week with broad weakness as sentiment swung from early optimism to profit-taking ahead of the Chinese New Year holiday.

The Hang Seng Index lost ground overall, slipping for much of the week before ending lower on Feb 13, with the index down about 1.7% at 26,567.12, pressured by selling in technology and China-linked stocks.

The week kicked off with upside momentum on Feb 9, as artificial intelligence and healthcare-related names attracted buying interest and helped lift broader sentiment. But as the sessions progressed, heavyweight tech shares, including major internet and cloud names, weakened, prompting investors to trim positions and driving a rotation into more defensive or cyclical segments.

Despite the overall pullback, market breadth wasn’t uniformly negative as a number of smaller stocks reached new 52-week highs by Feb 13, underscoring pockets of resilience amid the weakness. Two new listings also added fresh liquidity to the market during the week, although broader risk appetite remained subdued.

Trading volumes thinned as the holiday approached, with participants positioned cautiously ahead of the extended closure starting next week. Analysts see the near-term outlook as balanced, with investors watching for macro direction from mainland China and global markets once normal trading resumes.

data.title: Golds Slips To Below US$5000 As CNY Holiday Pauses Trade
data.description:
Gold slipped further below $5,000 an ounce in thin trading, with much of Asia closed for the Lunar New Year and after a US holiday on Monday.

Bullion fell as much as 1.4% on Tuesday, having lost 1% in the previous session. The metal had rallied briefly on Friday when modest US inflation data boosted the case for the Federal Reserve to trim interest rates. Lower borrowing costs are a tailwind for non-yielding precious metals.

A wave of speculative buying pushed a multiyear rally to breaking point in late January, with gold surging to a record above $5,595 an ounce. An abrupt, two-day rout at the turn of the month pulled the metal back to near $4,400, but it has since regained roughly half of its losses. Trading has been choppy since.

Many banks — including BNP Paribas SA, Deutsche Bank AG and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. — have forecast that prices will resume their upward trend, with the factors that underpinned gold’s steady ascent set to persist. These include heightened geopolitical tensions, questions over the Fed’s independence and a wider shift away from currencies and sovereign bonds.

data.title: Stock Today: Dayang Ent Gains 1.62% Despite Seasonal Weakness Outlook
data.description:
Dayang Enterprise Holdings Bhd traded higher in active midday trading, supported by continued analyst optimism over its contract visibility and maintenance-focused operations.

RHB Investment Bank Bhd and Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd have both maintained BUY calls on the group, with target prices of RM2.13 and RM1.91 respectively, citing resilient offshore maintenance demand and solid medium-term earnings visibility.

As of 2.51 pm, the stock was last done at RM1.88, up 3 sen or 1.62%, after trading between RM1.83 and RM1.88. The counter opened at RM1.85, with about 890,700 shares changing hands, compared with a last adjusted closing price of RM1.85.

Analysts noted that Dayang delivered FY25 core earnings broadly in line with expectations at about RM180 million to RM183 million, although profit declined year on year as vessel utilisation and topside maintenance work orders normalised from earlier peaks.

Quarterly earnings were also affected by monsoon conditions that limited offshore execution and reduced vessel utilisation.

Looking ahead, research houses expect activity to remain seasonally softer in the first quarter of 2026 before recovering from the second quarter onwards, supported by healthy maintenance and modification demand under Petronas’ activity outlook for 2026 to 2028.

Dayang’s outstanding call-out contracts of roughly RM4.8 billion to RM4.9 billion continue to provide revenue visibility, while its net cash position of about RM365 million is expected to support expansion opportunities across the oil and gas services value chain.

data.title: Dollar Holds Gains In Thin Trading As Market Awaits Fed Minutes, US GDP
data.description:
The dollar held gains on Tuesday as markets awaited signals, expected later this week, about the potential timing of rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.

The yen trimmed losses from a day earlier when worse-than-expected Japanese economic data stirred expectations that the government would ramp up stimulus. The Aussie dollar edged lower after the release of Reserve Bank of Australia’s minutes from its February meeting.

Trading was thin with many markets in Asia closed for the Lunar New Year holiday and following the President’s Day holiday in the U.S. Key economic events lie later in the week, with minutes from the Fed’s last meeting and advance figures on U.S. gross domestic product.

“We’re quite positive on the U.S. economy,” said Kristina Clifton, senior currency strategist at Commonwealth Bank of Australia in Sydney. “The market is currently pricing a high chance of a June interest rate cut, which is also our view. However, we differ from the market in that we expect a follow-up cut in July.”  

“We judge that the most important driver of the dollar through 2026 will be the narrative of U.S. exceptionalism,” she added.The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies, was little changed at 97.12, after a 0.2 per cent gain in the previous session. The euro fell 0.06 per cent to $1.1843.

The yen strengthened 0.15 per cent to 153.28 per dollar. Sterling weakened 0.07 per cent to $1.3616.

Data on Friday showed U.S. consumer prices increased less than expected in January, giving the Fed additional leeway for policy easing this year.

Money market traders are pricing in 62 basis points of easing for the rest of this year, implying two quarter-point cuts and about a 50 per cent chance of a third. The next cut is likely in June, with markets assigning an 80 per cent chance of a 25-basis-point reduction.

The Federal Open Market Committee issues minutes from its January meeting on Wednesday. Other key data points this week include inflation readings for Britain, Canada and Japan, as well as preliminary readings of global business activity on Friday.

A recent rally in the yen stalled on Monday when official figures showed Japan’s economy barely grew last quarter, eking out an annualised 0.2 per cent expansion.

The Australian dollar weakened 0.07 per cent versus the greenback to $0.7064. New Zealand’s kiwi weakened 0.08 per cent to $0.6026 ahead of the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s policy meeting on Wednesday, when it is widely expected to hold rates steady.

Australia’s central bank concluded inflation would stay stubbornly high if it had not hiked interest rates as it did this month and it was not yet sure if further tightening would be necessary.

Minutes of the RBA’s board meeting last month showed members were worried that the risks to its inflation and employment mandates had “shifted materially.” 

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin gained 0.05 per cent to $68,881.72, while ether was little changed at $1,999.11.

Reuters

data.title: Weekly Review: STI Hits Record High But Ends Week On Softer Note
data.description:
The benchmark Singapore Exchange closed the week of Feb 9-13 on a cautious footing after a volatile stretch that saw the Straits Times Index (STI) scale a fresh record before retreating on late profit-taking.

The STI rallied strongly in the first half of the week, briefly breaching the psychologically significant 5,000-point mark for the first time, lifted by gains in banking and blue-chip counters. Optimism over regional growth prospects and resilient corporate earnings underpinned buying momentum.

However, sentiment turned more measured heading into Feb 13 as investors locked in gains following the sharp run-up. Banking heavyweights led the pullback, dragging the index off its peak, while broader market breadth turned mixed.

Despite the late-week dip, overall trading activity remained firm, reflecting sustained institutional participation. Analysts expect near-term consolidation as investors digest recent gains and await fresh economic cues.

data.title: Profit-Taking Persists On Hang Seng Index, RHB
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a long trading bias on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), although the contract remains under pressure amid continued profit-taking and weakening momentum.

The research house noted that HSIF extended its losing streak last Friday, falling 456 points to close at 26,530 points after reversing from an intraday high of 27,120 points. The contract later edged up 30 points in the evening session to 26,560 points.

According to RHB Research, the latest price action, together with a declining relative strength index and trading below the 20-day simple moving average, indicates that sellers currently hold the technical advantage. Should the correction persist, the index may drift towards the 25,400-point support level.

However, a rebound could see HSIF move towards the 28,600-point resistance, followed by 29,100 points. The research house said the positive trading bias remains intact as long as the contract holds above the 26,400-point threshold, which also serves as the revised stop-loss level to manage downside risk.

Immediate support is seen at 26,400 points, with the next support at 25,500 points, while resistance levels are pegged at 28,600 points and 29,100 points.

data.title: Dollar Holds Firm As Rate-Cut Bets Build Ahead Of Fed Minutes
data.description:
The US dollar held its gains on Feb 17 in subdued trading, as investors looked ahead to minutes from the Federal Reserve and advance US gross domestic product data for signals on the timing of potential interest rate cuts.

Reuters reported that the dollar index was little changed at 97.12 after rising 0.2% in the previous session. The euro slipped 0.06% to US$1.1843, sterling eased 0.07% to US$1.3616, while the Japanese yen strengthened 0.15% to 153.28 per dollar.

Trading conditions were thin, with several Asian markets closed for the Lunar New Year holiday and US markets shut on Monday for Presidents’ Day.

Investor focus is firmly on the Federal Open Market Committee’s January meeting minutes due Wednesday. Money markets are pricing in around 62 basis points of easing this year — equivalent to two quarter-point cuts and roughly a 50% chance of a third. Traders see an 80% probability of a 25-basis-point rate reduction in June.

Kristina Clifton, senior currency strategist at Commonwealth Bank of Australia, said the bank expects a June cut followed by another move in July, adding that the narrative of US economic outperformance is likely to remain a key driver of the dollar through 2026.

Recent data showing US consumer prices rose less than expected in January has given policymakers additional leeway to ease monetary policy later this year.

In Asia-Pacific currencies, the Australian dollar slipped 0.07% to US$0.7064 after minutes from the Reserve Bank of Australia showed board members believed inflation would have remained stubbornly high without this month’s rate hike and were uncertain whether further tightening would be necessary.

The New Zealand dollar weakened 0.08% to US$0.6026 ahead of Wednesday’s policy decision by the Reserve Bank of New Zealand, where rates are widely expected to be held steady.

The yen’s earlier rally stalled after official data showed Japan’s economy expanded at an annualised pace of just 0.2% last quarter, fuelling expectations that authorities may step up stimulus efforts.

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin rose 0.05% to US$68,881.72, while ether was little changed at US$1,999.11, as broader markets awaited clearer direction from upcoming economic releases.

data.title: Asia Shares Becalmed By Holidays, Dire Japan Data
data.description:
Asian shares were quietly consolidating recent hefty gains on Monday as holidays made for thin trading, and dismal economic data out of Japan took some of the heat out of that booming market.

China, South Korea, Taiwan and the United States were among the centres off, leaving currencies, commodities and bonds all becalmed.

The major data of the week are not out until Friday when surveys of global manufacturing hit and the U.S. reports gross domestic product for the fourth quarter. Median forecasts are for annualised growth of 3.0%, down from 4.4% the previous quarter but still solid.

Japan on Monday reported its economy grew a miserly 0.1% annualised in the December quarter, far below the 1.6% gain forecast as government spending dragged on activity.

The disappointing figures underline the tough task ahead for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and should support her push for more aggressive fiscal stimulus.

Perhaps with that in mind, investors pushed Japan’s Nikkei up 0.2%, following a 5% rise last week. MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan firmed 0.1%.

South Korea’s tech-heavy market surged 8.2% last week, while Taiwan climbed almost 6% for the week.

data.title: Bursa Ends Streak On Lower Note As Investors Rein In On Profits
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the trading week on a softer note after a mixed set of sessions as investors took profits ahead of the upcoming Chinese New Year break and reacted to global cues.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) started the week with modest strength but trended lower as the week progressed. Early in the week, broader indices showed incremental gains, with the FBM Emas Index rising on Feb 9 before sentiment turned cautious in mid-week. By Feb 13, the FBM KLCI retreated 0.65% to close around 1,739.5, reflecting broad-based selling as losers outnumbered gainers across the market.

Trading activity remained subdued ahead of the holiday, with lighter turnover and a selective approach from investors. Profit-taking was evident, particularly in blue-chip names, as market participants locked in recent gains and positioned defensively.

On the ACE Market, small-cap and growth stocks exhibited mixed performance, with volume remaining uneven and overall indexes showing only marginal improvements over the week.

A notable event during the week was the strong debut of a newly listed counter on the ACE Market, where shares more than doubled on their first day of trading, underscoring pockets of speculative interest despite the broader cautious tone.

Market Outlook: Analysts expect the local bourse to remain in a consolidative mode in the short term as traders return from the holiday period and await fresh macroeconomic catalysts.

data.title: Stock Today: 99 Speed Mart Dips 6% Despite Strong FY25 Results
data.description:
99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd fell 6.33% to RM3.70, with 37.85 million shares changing hands as of 12.24 pm.

The stock opened at RM3.98, touched an intraday high of RM3.98 and a low of RM3.66, reflecting a pullback after a recent sharp run-up.

The decline comes after the company reported a strong set of results for the financial year ended 2025 (FY25). Core net profit rose 20.2% year-on-year to RM614 million, supported by aggressive store expansion, resilient demand for essential goods, and improved profit margins.

Revenue for FY25 increased 14.5% to RM5.3 billion, underpinned by higher same-store sales and a more optimised product mix.

A key driver of the growth was the addition of 259 net new outlets, a 9.3% increase in store count, lifting same-store sales growth to 7.8% for the full year. Operational efficiency and product mix optimisation helped boost gross profit margins to 11.4%, while operating income rose 15.3%.

The group’s effective tax rate fell slightly to 24.5%, supporting net profitability.

In the fourth quarter, core net profit climbed 22.8% year-on-year to RM157.3 million, with revenue up 19%, though quarter-on-quarter profit slipped 2% due to higher staff costs and promotional spending.

Analysts noted government cash assistance programmes, with around two-thirds of outlets participating, continue to drive footfall and spending among lower- and middle-income households.

Following the results, CIMB Group raised its target price for 99 Holdings to RM3.92 from RM3.65 but downgraded the stock to “Hold,” citing the recent 29.5% share rally that has largely priced in near-term earnings growth.

data.title: Stock Picks: Northeast Group And Autocount Dotcom
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Northeast Group Bhd and Autocount Dotcom Bhd as stocks with potential technical upside, supported by improving price momentum and bullish chart patterns.

RHB Research said Northeast Group’s uptrend may resume if the stock forms an interim base near its recent breakout point.

The counter is trading above the RM0.62 resistance level, indicating that buyers currently hold the technical advantage. Following a period of consolidation, the share price could advance towards RM0.69 and subsequently RM0.76.

However, a decline below the RM0.55 support level would invalidate the positive outlook and may trigger a correction phase.

Autocount Dotcom is also seen extending its gains after staging a bullish breakout on strong trading volume.

The stock formed a long bullish candlestick and closed above the RM0.70 resistance level, signalling strong upward momentum.

Post-breakout, the counter may test RM0.75, with a further potential move towards RM0.80. A fall below RM0.65 would indicate the start of a corrective trend, according to the research house.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Firmer Against US Dollar, Tracking Positive Momentum On 4Q25 GDP Growth
data.description:
The ringgit strengthened against the US dollar at the opening on Monday, tracking positive momentum on the better-than-expected fourth quarter (4Q) 2025 gross domestic product (GDP) growth of 6.3 per cent which was announced last Friday, an analyst said. 

At 8am, the ringgit opened firmer at 3.8970/9190 against the greenback, compared to 3.9060/9155 at last Friday’s closing. 

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the current official GDP forecast for 2026 of 4.0 per cent to 4.5 per cent is likely to be revised upward to account for the country’s resilient economy amidst external uncertainties.

“This would mean the Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) would be kept steady in 2026 as there is no urgency to overstimulate the economy,” he told Bernama. 

Mohd Afzanizam said the interest rate gap between the US Federal Reserve (Fed) fund rate and the OPR would be narrowed as the Fed is expected to ease its monetary policy stance this year. 

Therefore, he said the ringgit-dollar (exchange rate) would make another attempt to break the psychological level of RM3.9000 today.

At the opening, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.

It appreciated against the euro to 4.6242/6503 from 4.6325/6390 at Friday’s close, and rose vis-à-vis the British pound to 5.3178/3479 from 5.3211/3286 last Friday, but depreciated versus the Japanese yen to 2.5501/5648 from 2.5435/5474.

The local note traded higher against its ASEAN peers.

The ringgit inched up versus the Singapore dollar to 3.0860/1042 from 3.0904/0950 and surged versus the Thai baht at 12.5273/6200 from 12.5607/5849.

It also gained against the Indonesian rupiah to 231.4/232.8 from 231.9/232.4 at Friday’s close, and was higher against the Philippine peso at 6.71/6.76 from 6.73/6.74.

Bernama

data.title: 99 Speed Mart Decline Marks Heaviest Volume Trade
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia was mostly subdued by midday, with key indexes edging lower.

The FBMKLCI slipped 3.68 points, or 0.21%, to 1,735.86, while the broader FBMEMAS eased 17.41 points, or 0.14%, to 12,720.79.

The FBM70 held steady at 17,565.37, down just 0.02%, and the F4GBM declined 1.29 points, or 0.13%.

In contrast, the FBMSHA bucked the trend, gaining 24.30 points, or 0.20%, to 12,246.31.

Among individual stocks, 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Berhad (5326) was a notable laggard, falling 6.33% to RM3.70 on 37.89 million shares traded, reflecting profit-taking after recent gains.

UTDPLT (2089) also retreated, down 1.26% to RM29.80, while BKAWAN (1899), HEIM (3255) and Allianz (1163) recorded modest losses.

On the upside, Nestle (4707) rose 0.92% to RM110.00 and MPI (3867) gained 1.34% to RM30.18. MNRB (6459) advanced 10.97% to RM2.73, Petronas Gas (6033) added 1.32% to RM18.46, and Tenaga Nasional (5347) inched up 1.29% to RM14.14.

Trading activity remained concentrated among small- and mid-cap counters, with HONGSENG (0041) topping volume at 128.05 million shares, followed by Zetrix (0138) with 37.99 million and 99 Speed Mart with 37.89 million.

Market watchers noted that defensive and essential goods counters were providing some support, even as broader sentiment remained cautious amid mixed economic signals.
